response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger an allergic reaction in some individuals  
the human body has mechanisms to detect and respond to allergens such as peanuts  
the immune system of someone allergic to peanuts may react negatively to the presence of peanuts in any environment including underwater  
the composition of peanuts does not change with the environment and therefore the allergic reaction can occur in any setting  

Sec. 2  
the physical properties of water do not affect the allergenic proteins in peanuts  
the allergic reaction is based on the immune system recognizing the peanut proteins as foreign  
the presence of water does not alter the chemical structure of the proteins in peanuts  
the allergic reaction is not influenced by the environment and remains consistent regardless of the setting
